mission.title: Install and Configure Kind (Kubernetes in Docker)
mission.description: Kind (Kubernetes in Docker) is a tool for running local Kubernetes clusters using Docker container nodes. It is primarily designed for testing Kubernetes itself but can also be used for local development or CI. Key trade-offs include the requirement for Docker and the fact that it's not suitable for production workloads.

steps.title: Install Kind Binary
steps.description:
This step installs the Kind binary directly from the official release URL, ensuring you have the correct version for your platform.

```bash

[ $(uname -m) = x86_64 ] && curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-$(uname)-amd64

[ $(uname -m) = aarch64 ] && curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-$(uname)-arm64

chmod +x ./kind

sudo mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind

```

> For AMD64 / x86_64 on Linux
> For ARM64 on Linux
> Make the Kind binary executable
> Move Kind to a directory in your PATH

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a local Kubernetes cluster using Kind with the default configuration.

```bash

kind create cluster --image kindest/node:v1.31.0

```

> Creates a cluster with Kubernetes version 1.31.0

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step ensures that your kubeconfig is set up to interact with the Kind cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=$(kind get kubeconfig-path --name=

```

> Set KUBECONFIG to the path of the Kind cluster's kubeconfig

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks that the nodes in your Kind cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Verify that the nodes are in 'Ready' state

uninstall.title: Delete Kind Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Kind cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all workloads and data in the cluster.

```bash

kind delete cluster

```

> This will remove the cluster and all its resources

uninstall.title: Remove Kind Binary
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Kind binary from your system. Ensure you no longer need Kind before proceeding.

```bash

sudo rm /usr/local/bin/kind

```

> Remove the Kind binary from the PATH

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kind
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup any important data from your cluster. This step upgrades Kind to the latest version.

```bash

curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-$(uname)-amd64

chmod +x ./kind

sudo mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind

```

> Download the latest Kind binary
> Make the new Kind binary executable
> Replace the old Kind binary with the new one

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kind: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kind binary is not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Ensure that /usr/local/bin is in your PATH or move the Kind binary to a directory that is in your PATH.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: failed to start nodes'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Docker is not running or not properly configured.

**Fix:**
Ensure Docker is installed and running. Check Docker's status with 'docker ps'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster creation failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources available on the host machine.

**Fix:**
Ensure your machine meets the minimum resource requirements for Kind (at least 2 vCPU and 4GB RAM).

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubelet is unhealthy'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes node is not starting correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the node with 'kind get logs <node-name>' to diagnose the issue.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31, improved cluster creation speed.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required when upgrading from v0.30.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get nodes' showing your Kind cluster nodes in 'Ready' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure Docker is installed and running on your machine.
